content.problemStatement:
The alternating sum of an array AAA of length NNN is defined to be

‚ąĎi=1N(‚ąí1)i+1Ai\sum_{i=1}^N (-1)^{i+1} A_ii=1‚ąĎN‚Äč(‚ąí1)i+1Ai‚ÄčIf you expand it out, this is the expression A1‚ąíA2+A3‚ąíA4+‚Ä¶+(‚ąí1)N+1ANA_1 - A_2 + A_3 - A_4 + \ldots + (-1)^{N+1} A_NA1‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč+A3‚Äč‚ąíA4‚Äč+‚Ä¶+(‚ąí1)N+1AN‚Äč.

You're given an array AAA containing NNN integers. You can rearrange this array however you like.

Find the **maximum** possible value of the alternating sum of AAA after rearrangement.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the maximum possible alternating sum after rearranging AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One optimal rearrangement is [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3], which gives an alternating sum of 2‚ąí1+3=42 - 1 + 3 = 42‚ąí1+3=4.
Test case 222: No matter how the array is rearranged, its alternating sum is 5‚ąí5+5‚ąí5=05-5+5-5 = 05‚ąí5+5‚ąí5=0.
Test case 333: One optimal rearrangement is [4,3,9,1,5,1][4, 3, 9, 1, 5, 1][4,3,9,1,5,1], which gives an alternating sum of 4‚ąí3+9‚ąí1+5‚ąí1=134-3+9-1+5-1 = 134‚ąí3+9‚ąí1+5‚ąí1=13.
